topic: The One Prompt That Fixes Claude's Writing

caption.hook: The one prompt that fixes Claude's robotic writing 🧵
caption.summary: Claude pads, hedges and leans on the same AI words by default — 'delve', 'tapestry', 'in conclusion'. Readers feel it instantly. This one rewrite prompt fixes it in a single paste.
caption.content:
The prompt:
"Rewrite the text below. Strip every filler phrase, hedge and AI-ism. Use short, concrete sentences and vary their length so it sounds human. Keep my facts exact. Return only the edited text."

Pro tip: add one line — 'Write it for a busy founder who skims.' — and it gets sharper.
caption.cta: Save this + follow @genzcapital for daily AI tools & how-tos. No fluff. No hype.

slides.headline: The One Prompt That Fixes Claude's Writing
Claude Sounds Like A Robot By Default
slides.body: Out of the box it hedges, over-explains and leans on the same AI words — 'delve', 'tapestry', 'in conclusion'. Readers feel it instantly.

slides.headline: It's Trained To Be Safe, Not Sharp
slides.body: Models default to padded, neutral prose. You have to tell it to cut the fluff — it won't do it on its own.

slides.headline: Paste This Before Your Draft
slides.body: Rewrite the text below. Strip filler, hedges and AI-isms. Short concrete sentences. Vary length so it sounds human. Keep my facts exact. Return only the edited text.
slides.ref_query: dark screen glowing cursor command line

slides.headline: Same Idea. Half The Words.
slides.body: Before: 'In today's fast-paced world it is important to delve into…' After: 'Here's what actually matters.' That's the whole difference.
slides.ref_query: messy paper versus clean minimal desk

slides.headline: Tell It Who You Are Writing For
slides.body: Add one line: 'Write it for a busy founder who skims.' The prompt gets sharper the second Claude knows the reader.
slides.ref_query: young founder reading phone walking office

slides.headline: What You Just Learned
points: Claude pads and hedges by default — you have to fix it.
Paste the 'sound human' rewrite prompt before any draft.
Force short, concrete, varied sentences; ban AI-isms.
Name the reader so the tone matches who's reading.
